vision_report.compositional_truth_check: The registry is largely invalidated by the visual evidence. P1, P2, P3, and P4 are not identifiable as living specimens in the provided sequence. The pots appear to contain only soil and debris.

inventory_reconciliation.P1: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible.
inventory_reconciliation.P2: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible.
inventory_reconciliation.P3: Systemic Loss: White rabbit anchor present, but no Pothos visible.
inventory_reconciliation.P4: Systemic Loss: No plant matter visible.

plant_audit.P3: White rabbit anchor present; soil surface appears undisturbed but devoid of foliage.

biome_observations.soil_texture: Appears consistently dark and potentially desiccated across all frames.
biome_observations.debris: White fragments (possibly eggshells or perlite) are present in the soil of the foreground pot.
biome_observations.surface: The desk surface is dark and featureless.

temporal_deltas.EARLIEST_to_T-2: No significant change; pots remain empty of visible vegetation.
temporal_deltas.T-1_to_CURRENT: Total loss of visual data; the environment has transitioned to a state of complete darkness/blackout.

vision_report.visual_health_inference: The biome is in a state of total failure. There is no evidence of photosynthetic activity or living plant tissue. The system is effectively dormant or deceased.
vision_report.anomalies: The transition to total darkness in the final two images suggests a failure of the fixed camera LED or a complete power loss to the observation environment.
vision_report.narrative_description: The audit reveals a catastrophic loss of the botanical specimens. From the earliest image, the pots were already devoid of recognizable plant life, containing only soil and minor debris. The white rabbit anchor in P3 remains, but the Pothos is absent. The final two images show a complete black-out, rendering further visual analysis impossible and confirming a total cessation of the observation environment's functionality.
